fact: The first official cricket Test match was played in 1877 between Australia and England at the Melbourne Cricket Ground (MCG). Australia won by 45 runs.
In 2010, Sachin Tendulkar became the first male cricketer to score a double century (200 runs) in a One Day International (ODI) match, achieving this against South Africa in Gwalior.
The inaugural ICC Men's T20 World Cup was held in 2007 in South Africa. India won the tournament by defeating Pakistan by 5 runs in a thrilling final at Johannesburg.
The Thomas Cup, established in 1948, is the premier international men's team badminton championship. India won its historic first title in 2022 by defeating Indonesia 3-0.
Prakash Padukone became the first Indian to win the prestigious All England Open Badminton Championships in 1980, defeating Liem Swie King of Indonesia in the final.
The FIFA World Cup was first held in 1930. Uruguay hosted and won the tournament, defeating Argentina 4-2 in the final match in Montevideo.
Brazil holds the record for the most FIFA World Cup titles, winning the tournament five times in the years 1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, and 2002.
The UEFA Champions League, originally known as the European Champion Clubs' Cup, was introduced in 1955. Real Madrid won the first five consecutive tournaments.
The National Basketball Association (NBA) was founded in 1946 as the Basketball Association of America (BAA) before merging with the National Basketball League (NBL) in 1949.
Wilt Chamberlain holds the NBA single-game scoring record, scoring 100 points for the Philadelphia Warriors in a victory against the New York Knicks on March 2, 1692.
The Wimbledon Championships, founded in 1877, is the oldest tennis tournament in the world. It is the only Grand Slam event currently played on traditional grass courts.
In 2023, Novak Djokovic broke the men's all-time record by winning his 24th Grand Slam singles title at the US Open, surpassing Rafael Nadal's tally of 22.
Usain Bolt of Jamaica set the current men's 100-meter world record time of 9.58 seconds at the World Athletics Championships in Berlin on August 16, 2009.
